title: Kohlrauschia glumacea
taxonomy: Kohlrauschia glumacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a member of the order Malpighiales. The classification of this plant is further broken down into the class Magnoliopsida, subclass Rosidae, and clade rosid. The family Euphorbiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, many of which are known for their milky sap. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Kohlrauschia glumacea.

intro: Kohlrauschia glumacea, also known as the Thai star anise tree, is a plant species in the family Annonaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Kohlrauschia glumacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
